response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, well-rounded engineer with 15+ years of experience and a clear upward trajectory to staff-level roles, but the technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with this position. The role demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on Spring and enterprise framework experience — none of which feature meaningfully in his resume. His primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js, with DLang and some C/C++. His closest relevant touchpoints are writing C components for a C IDE at National Instruments and developer tooling work at Google, but neither bridges the gap to JVM-based IDE engineering.
strengths: Staff-level engineer at Google with demonstrated developer tooling experience including MCP code generators and agentic infrastructure
Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE, showing some exposure to language tooling contexts
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source contributions, and community knowledge-sharing
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise; Java appearances in the resume are incidental (robotic arms, mobile orchestration) with no enterprise or JVM-depth context
Zero Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume
Core technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — a fundamental stack mismatch with the JVM tooling focus of this role
